birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: Approximately 60 to 70% of the entire razorbill population breeds in Iceland. Some razorbill colonies include (north to south): * Hornøya, Norway (70°23' N) - 500 pairs. * Grímsey, Iceland (66°33' N) * Látrabjarg, Iceland (65°30' N) - 230,000 pairs, about 40% of the global population (mid-1990s estimate)
